Base Fee covers the minimum costs for the Oracle resources at a specific customer site (e.g., a 
single data center or IT facility) and the Add-On Rack Fees cover the additional costs associated 
with the number of racks the customer has purchased for that site. In addition, if the customer 
has additional data centers or IT facilities that can be managed remotely by that on-site Oracle 
resource, only the Add-On Fees need to be applied to the racks in the remote site or sites (i.e., 
no Base Fee is required since the remote sites do not require on-site resources from Oracle).
Examples: 
Customer has 2 data centers: Data Center A and Data Center B and each data center has 1 
full rack (for a total of 2 full racks).
Data Center A and Data Center B each require Oracle on-site management. In this example, 
the customer would be charged the Base Fee plus the Add-On Full Rack Fee for each data 
center (i.e., charged twice for the Base Fee and charged twice for the Add-On Full Rack Fee ). 
However, if only Data Center A requires Oracle on-site management and Data Center B can be 
remotely managed by the same on-site Oracle resource in Data Center A, in this example, the 
customer would be charged the Base Fee and the Add-On Full Rack Fee for Data Center A 
and just the Add-On Full Rack Fee for Data Center B.
NOTE: Discounting greater than 0% for either of the following two services requires HQAPP 
approval:
- Oracle Public Cloud Machine - Disconnected Mode 24x7 - Base Fee
- Oracle Public Cloud Machine - Disconnected Mode 8x5 - Base Fee

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS AND PREREQUISITES
Base Fee covers the minimum costs for the Oracle resources at a specific customer site (e.g., a 
single data center or IT facility) and the Add-On Rack Fees cover the additional costs associated 
with the number of racks the customer has purchased for that site. In addition, if the customer 
has additional data centers or IT facilities that can be managed remotely by that on-site Oracle 
resource, only the Add-On Fees need to be applied to the racks in the remote site or sites (i.e., 
no Base Fee is required since the remote sites do not require on-site resources from Oracle).
Examples: 
Customer has 2 data centers: Data Center A and Data Center B and each data center has 1 
full rack (for a total of 2 full racks).
Data Center A and Data Center B each require Oracle on-site management. In this example, 
the customer would be charged the Base Fee plus the Add-On Full Rack Fee for each data 
center (i.e., charged twice for the Base Fee and charged twice for the Add-On Full Rack Fee ). 
However, if only Data Center A requires Oracle on-site management and Data Center B can be 
remotely managed by the same on-site Oracle resource in Data Center A, in this example, the 
customer would be charged the Base Fee and the Add-On Full Rack Fee for Data Center A 
and just the Add-On Full Rack Fee for Data Center B.
